name: Prototype — Act 1 layout
_doc: Act 1 layout per newGameDesign/PROTOTYPE_PLAN.md §4 typical playthrough. PR #4.5 South Logistics Belt redesign: north reserved for combat + chop + walls; south holds the worker infrastructure in two rows (storages at z=+5, worker/builder bases at z=+7). Military pads moved north of the player (z=-4) so spawned soldiers already face the zombie horde. 12 zombies pre-placed across 3 ring distances. 4 wall ghosts (1 active in State B, 1 more activated in D, the remaining 2 reserved for Act 2's State E). 2 spawn pads (Scout + Bruiser) hidden until State C.
entities: _doc 12 zombies pre-placed at the spawn area (~z=-19 to -26, north). 5 marchers commit to chasing the player from boot (permanentChase EnemyAI flag); other 7 wander idle near spawn until the player approaches their aggroRadius. Per PROTOTYPE_PLAN §4 typical playthrough: '12 zombies pre-placed; 5 aggro on player.'
_doc 5 marchers at z=-19, spread east-west — walk south at speed 4 → reach player (z=0) in ~4.75s
_doc 7 idlers at z=-22 to -26, scattered — wander idle near spawn (outside aggroRadius 8)

_doc 6 trees clustered ~10-14 tiles north of player for wood gathering. Trees at z=-12 / -14 are outside the perimeter and become unreachable once the north fence is built — they'll be reachable again once the auto-sink-logs feature lands.

_doc 4 INSIDE-PERIMETER trees so the staged build (D/E/F) can finish without auto-sink. Move/remove these once auto-sink lets the player reach the outer trees again. South pair moved from z=+5 to z=+3 to make room for the south logistics belt at z=+5..+7.

_doc 4 rock nodes flanking the base east/west, just outside the fence at x=±12, z=±3. Stone is rarer than wood (4 rocks vs 6 trees) and lives on the unused east/west axis to diversify player movement. Inside the fence is reserved for the existing belt; rocks are 4u outside the east/west fences (x=±8) so they're freely reachable in Act 1 and remain accessible once the east/west walls + auto-sink gates land in Act 2.

_doc TEST: 4 Scouts + 1 Bruiser + 1 Sharpshooter pre-spawned in a skirmish line north of the player so we can watch all three classes fight side by side. Sharpshooter sits one row back at z=-1.5 since its 15u range lets it shoot over the front line into the zombie horde. Remove once balancing is locked.

_doc PR #4.0 — Acts 1-3 architecture redesign (2026-05-07). Workers pre-placed at boot per the locked design pivot: 1 wood-worker, 1 essence-collector, 2 builders all on the map from frame 0. Storage props (wood + essence, cap 100) are visible at fixed positions; workers deposit there, builders withdraw. The old worker-pad-active test is gone.

_doc PR #4.5 South Logistics Belt — all worker infrastructure on the southern third of the perimeter so the north stays clean for combat + tree-chop + wall-build action. Two rows: storages forward at z=+5 (visible stacks read at a glance), worker/builder bases tucked behind the south wall ghost at z=+7. Wood ops on the west side, essence ops on the east, builder centered for equal reach to all 4 wall ghosts.

_doc Stone storage archetype + preset are kept in code as scaffolding for the future stone-worker pipeline, but NOT placed in the prototype scene yet — the prototype doesn't ship a stone economy. Re-enable by adding {"archetype":"stone-storage","position":{"x":0,"y":0,"z":3}} here.
_doc Storage row at z=+5 — visible stacks face the player.
_doc Worker base row at z=+7 — wood-W west, essence-C east, builder centered. Each worker base sits ~3.6u from its storage (short deposit trip).
_fenceDoc: 20u × 20u palisade-log enclosure with staged build. Each side is hidden at boot; filling its unlock-zone fires `zone:built` with the side tag, which main.js maps to `fence:revealSide`, lazily showing logs + creating colliders. Order in Act 1: B → north, C (army), D → south. Act 2 adds east + west.
_spawnNote: PrototypeStateMachine.entryActions of each state call EnemySystem.setConfig() with prototypeStates.json spawnConfigs. Pre-placed enemies above are counted toward maxAlive; spawner adds new ones once kills drop the count below the cap.
_wallZoneDoc: 4 fence-side build zones (one per cardinal direction). Each is a build-type drain pad with no archetype — on completion it emits zone:built with its tag, which main.js maps to fence:revealSide for that side. Pads sit ~7u inside the perimeter so the player drains from inside their base.

unlockZones._doc: North wall zone — Act 1 state B. Drains 10W+3E to raise the north palisade.
South wall zone — Act 1 state D.
East wall zone — Act 1 state E (test extension).
West wall zone — Act 1 state F (test extension).

Scout Pad — Green Military Base. Drains 4E to spawn 1 Scout. Activated in State C. PR #4.4: swapped to the military-base-green mesh + snappy auto-drop tuning (drainRate 0.05 = ~80ms per essence, range 2.5). PR #4.5: moved north of player (cell [13,11] = world (-8,-4)) so spawned scouts are already in the combat zone facing zombies.

Bruiser Pad — Red Military Base. Drains 8E to spawn 1 Bruiser. Activated in State C. Same auto-drop tuning as green. PR #4.5: moved north of player to mirror Scout Pad — east flank in the combat zone.

_unlockZonesDoc: PR #4.0 — Worker Pad ghost zone removed. Workers are now pre-placed at boot (see entities[]). Old test_zone_pr33 builder test also removed; the builder now targets the four real wall ghosts (B/D/E/F).
